rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK VS. LISA GRANT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entering Judgment Against Defendant Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 08, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK's Motion For Entering Judgment Against Defendant Pursuant To Ccp 664.6. Granted. The court grants the motion to enter judgment against defendant in the amount of $9237.50. No opposition was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Upon receipt of the proposed order, the Court will sign the proposed Judgment that was submitted with the moving papers.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 08, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT LAWRENCE EBRINGER's Moion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement. The motion is granted. The $650,000 settlement is within the ballpark under Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. In reaching this decision, the Court has not considered any references to the substance of the mediation proceedings. Coldwell Banker fails to demonstrate good cause for a continuance. rulings.Case Title: GINELLE CLOAR VS. KELLY MAZZUCCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Kelly Mazzucco, Thomas Mazzucco'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Of Dmv Medical Records Of Defendant Kelly Mazzucco And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 2, Defendants Kelly Mazzucco, Thomas Mazzucco'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Of Dmv Medical Records Of Defendant Kelly Mazzucco And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Defendant's motion to quash the subpoenas is GRANTED insofar as it relates to the subpoena for DMV records and the subpoena issued directly to Dr. Laxer. The Defendant raised the issue of the subpoena to the Sutter Pacific Medical Foundation for the first time in her Reply papers. Thus, that subpoena is not properly before this Court and will not be considered as part of this motion. The motion for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant to prepare proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GINELLE CLOAR VS. KELLY MAZZUCCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Enforce Subpoena To Compel The Release Of Deft'S Dmv Records And Specific Medical Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF GINELLE CLOAR Motion To Enforce Subpoena To Compel The Release Of Defendant's Dmv Records And Specific Medical Records. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Plaintiff's motion to compel production of DMV records is DENIED. The Plaintiff has failed to show that the Defendant's general driving and accident history is relevant to any of the claims or defenses in this case. Additionally, the Plaintiff has the burden to show that the Defendant's medical records, if any, contained in the DMV file are directly relevant to any of the claims or defenses in this case, and that the subpoena for said records is narrowly drawn. See Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 859. The Plaintiff has failed to meet this burden. For the same reasons, the Plaintiff's motion to compel production of medical records from Kenneth D. Laxer, MD is DENIED. The Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant to prepare proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY RAY RICHARDSON et al VS. PAUL SIMON NOUNOU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Paul Nounou'S Motion For Order Finding Attorney Abused Discovery Process, Order To Compel Responses, Order To Appoint A Discovery Referre
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 3, Defendant Paul Nounou's Motion For Order Finding Attorney Abused Discovery Process, Order To Compel Responses, Order To Appoint A Discovery Referee. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part without prejudice. The deposition of Plaintiff Ray Richardson shall be reconvened at a time and place mutually convenient to the parties. The meet and confer efforts made on the record during the deposition were sufficient. See Stewart v. Colonial Western Agency, Inc. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 1006, 1015-17; Townsend v. Superior Court (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1438. The objections to the questions set forth in the Defendant's separate statement are overruled. Attorney Kaplan is ordered to limit his objections to the form of the question only and refrain from additional remarks directed either at opposing counsel or his own client. If an objection is based on privilege, it is to be stated clearly and succinctly and if the witness is instructed not to answer, the instruction is to be stated clearly and succinctly without additional commentary. Defendant's request for the appointment of a discovery referee is DENIED without prejudice. Should attorney Kaplan's conduct at this deposition continue, or be repeated at any subsequent deposition in this case, the Court will entertain a renewed request for the appointment of a discovery referee with the costs of the referee to be borne by the Plaintiff. See Cal. Code Civ. Proc. §§ 639, 645.1. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant to prepare proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RUBEN TORRES VS. BENIHANA NATIONAL CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 08, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RUBEN TORRES' Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions. The motion to compel further responses is granted as to special interrogatories numbers 8, 9, and 32. Following meet and confer exchanges, the requested information was limited as to time and place. Special interrogatory number 8 seeks the identity of employees who worked the same shift with plaintiff. Special interrogatory number 9 was subsequently limited by the parties to employees who worked the same San Francisco location as plaintiff during the four year period of his employment. Special interrogatory number 32 seeks a 'yes" or "no" answer to a question concerning the actions of the employer. As to the privacy objections, employee identity information is specifically discoverable. See Puerto v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1242; Crab Addison v. Superior Court (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 958. The identity of the employees does not constitute a significant intrusion into the employee's reasonable expectation of privacy. See Pioneer Electronics (USA), Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 40 Cal.4th 360. Respondent party has not met its burden to demonstrate equal availability of the information under CCP sections 2030.220(c) and 2030.230. While defendant admits that it furnished copies of the plaintiff's wage statements, those statements do not contain sufficient information concerning the split shift. The requests for sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)
